cards.example: The company moved its headquarters to larger quarters downtown.

cards.definition: a question or request for information
cards.example: Direct any billing queries to the finance department.

cards.definition: doubtful in quality, honesty, or appropriateness
cards.example: The accountant flagged several questionable transactions for review.

cards.definition: the act of asking questions to obtain information
cards.example: The panel began their questioning of the job candidates.

cards.definition: a set of written questions used to gather information
cards.example: Please complete the customer satisfaction questionnaire.

cards.definition: a line of people or items waiting to be processed
cards.example: There is a long queue of orders waiting to be fulfilled.

cards.definition: at a fast speed; without delay
cards.example: Customer complaints should be resolved as quickly as possible.

cards.definition: to a considerable or moderate extent
cards.example: The renovated office space is quite spacious.

cards.definition: a limited quantity or target amount to be achieved
cards.example: The sales team exceeded its monthly quota by fifteen percent.

cards.definition: a formal statement of the price for goods or services
cards.example: We received a competitive quotation from the new supplier.

cards.definition: a formal estimate of the cost for a service or product
cards.example: The contractor provided a quote for the office renovation.

cards.definition: relating to official rules and regulations
cards.example: All products must meet strict regulatory requirements.

cards.definition: to repay someone for expenses they have incurred
cards.example: Employees will be reimbursed for approved travel expenses.

cards.definition: consistently good in quality or performance
cards.example: We need a reliable courier service for international shipments.

cards.definition: in an unwilling or hesitant manner
cards.example: The manager reluctantly approved the additional budget request.

cards.definition: the part that is left over after other parts are removed
cards.example: The remainder of the budget will be allocated to training.

cards.definition: the act of restoring a building to good condition
cards.example: The hotel will be closed during the renovation period.

cards.definition: having a good reputation; trustworthy
cards.example: We only work with reputable suppliers and distributors.

cards.definition: separately and in the order already mentioned
cards.example: Sales in Tokyo and Osaka grew by five and eight percent respectively.

cards.definition: a person who replies to a survey or questionnaire
cards.example: Over seventy percent of respondents rated the service as excellent.

cards.definition: to organize something differently; to reorganize
cards.example: The company plans to restructure its management team.

cards.definition: the sale of goods directly to consumers
cards.example: She has over ten years of experience in the retail industry.

cards.definition: to continue to keep or hold on to
cards.example: The company works hard to retain its most talented employees.

cards.definition: to get something back or recover data
cards.example: You can retrieve archived documents from the company database.

cards.definition: income generated from business activities
cards.example: Annual revenue exceeded expectations for the second year in a row.

cards.definition: changed or amended from an earlier version
cards.example: The revised schedule will take effect next Monday.

cards.definition: to arrange a time for an event or meeting
cards.example: We need to schedule a follow-up meeting for next week.

cards.definition: to obtain or guarantee something
cards.example: The team managed to secure funding for the new project.

cards.definition: a person's name written in their own handwriting
cards.example: Your signature is required at the bottom of the contract.

cards.definition: at the same time as something else
cards.example: The product launch will occur simultaneously in three countries.

cards.definition: by a small amount or degree
cards.example: Sales figures increased slightly compared to last quarter.

cards.definition: having a lot of room or space
cards.example: The new office is much more spacious than the old one.

cards.definition: to focus on a particular area of work or study
cards.example: Our firm specializes in international trade law.

cards.definition: a detailed description of design or material requirements
cards.example: The product must meet all specifications outlined in the contract.

cards.definition: a person with an interest in a business or project
cards.example: All stakeholders were invited to attend the quarterly review.

cards.definition: a plan designed to achieve a long-term goal
cards.example: The board approved a new strategy to expand into European markets.

cards.definition: to make a process more efficient and simpler
cards.example: We plan to streamline our ordering process next month.

cards.definition: strict, precise, and demanding in standards
cards.example: The factory must comply with stringent safety regulations.

cards.definition: to present a document or proposal for review
cards.example: Please submit your expense report by Friday.

cards.definition: a regular payment to receive a product or service
cards.example: The annual subscription includes access to all online courses.

cards.definition: a company controlled by a larger parent company
cards.example: The subsidiary reported strong growth in the Asian market.

cards.definition: large in amount, value, or importance
cards.example: The merger resulted in a substantial increase in market share.

cards.definition: enough to meet a particular need or purpose
cards.example: Please ensure there are sufficient supplies for the conference.

cards.definition: a person who oversees and directs the work of others
cards.example: Each new employee is assigned a supervisor during training.

cards.definition: something added to complete or enhance a thing
cards.example: The report includes a supplement with detailed financial data.

cards.definition: a company that provides goods or materials to others
cards.example: We are currently negotiating terms with a new supplier.

cards.definition: to exceed or go beyond a limit or expectation
cards.example: Revenue this year surpassed our initial projections.

cards.definition: an amount left over after needs are met
cards.example: The budget surplus will be allocated to research and development.

cards.definition: a study of opinions or experiences of a group
cards.example: The customer satisfaction survey revealed areas for improvement.

cards.definition: to temporarily stop or delay an activity
cards.example: Production was suspended until the safety inspection was completed.

cards.definition: able to be maintained over a long period
cards.example: The company is committed to sustainable manufacturing practices.

cards.definition: to count or calculate a total amount
cards.example: Please tally the votes from the board meeting.

cards.definition: a tax or duty on imported or exported goods
cards.example: New tariffs on steel imports affected several manufacturers.

cards.definition: not certain or fixed; subject to change
cards.example: We have set a tentative date for the product launch in May.

cards.definition: in an uncertain or provisional manner
cards.example: The merger has been tentatively scheduled for the third quarter.

cards.definition: the period of time a position is held
cards.example: During her tenure as director, sales grew by forty percent.

cards.definition: to end or bring something to a conclusion
cards.example: The company decided to terminate the contract early.

cards.definition: a statement praising a product or service
cards.example: Client testimonials are featured on our updated website.

cards.definition: complete and careful with attention to every detail
cards.example: A thorough review of the proposal is expected before approval.

cards.definition: in a complete and careful manner
cards.example: Each application is thoroughly reviewed by the hiring committee.

cards.definition: to grow, develop, or succeed vigorously
cards.example: Small businesses can thrive in a supportive economic environment.

cards.definition: a schedule showing when tasks should be completed
cards.example: The project timeline has been extended by two weeks.

cards.definition: done or occurring at a suitable or appropriate time
cards.example: Timely delivery of materials is critical for the project.

cards.definition: a legally registered symbol, word, or name for a product
cards.example: The company filed a trademark application for its new logo.

cards.definition: an instance of buying, selling, or exchanging something
cards.example: All transactions above ten thousand dollars require manager approval.

cards.definition: to move someone or something to another place or role
cards.example: She was transferred to the London office last month.

cards.definition: the process of changing from one state to another
cards.example: The transition to the new software will take approximately six weeks.

cards.definition: open, honest, and easy to understand
cards.example: The company maintains a transparent pricing policy for all clients.

cards.definition: to identify and fix problems in a system or process
cards.example: The IT department was called in to troubleshoot the network issue.

cards.definition: a fee charged for instruction or training
cards.example: The company offers tuition reimbursement for approved courses.

cards.definition: the rate at which employees leave a company
cards.example: High turnover has been a persistent challenge for the department.

cards.definition: fully agreed upon by all people involved
cards.example: The board reached a unanimous decision to approve the merger.

cards.definition: to estimate something as less than its actual value
cards.example: We underestimated the demand for the new product.

cards.definition: to commit to and begin a task or project
cards.example: The firm will undertake a comprehensive market analysis.

cards.definition: never done or known before
cards.example: The company achieved unprecedented growth in the fourth quarter.

cards.definition: to show or reveal something publicly for the first time
cards.example: The CEO will unveil the new product line at the conference.

cards.definition: happening or appearing soon
cards.example: Details about the upcoming workshop will be sent via email.

cards.definition: to improve something to a higher standard or version
cards.example: We plan to upgrade all employee laptops before the end of the year.

cards.definition: in a way that requires immediate action or attention
cards.example: The safety issue needs to be addressed urgently.

cards.definition: a basic service such as electricity, water, or gas
cards.example: Monthly utility costs for the building have risen sharply.

cards.definition: to make practical or effective use of something
cards.example: The department should utilize the new software to track inventory.

cards.definition: an unoccupied position or available room
cards.example: There is a vacancy for a senior analyst in the finance team.

cards.definition: legally or officially acceptable and effective
cards.example: Employees must carry a valid identification badge at all times.

cards.definition: to confirm something is true or accurate
cards.example: Please verify the shipping address before dispatch.

cards.definition: capable of working successfully; feasible
cards.example: The committee is exploring viable alternatives to reduce costs.

cards.definition: an act that breaks a law, rule, or agreement
cards.example: The safety violation resulted in a temporary shutdown of the plant.

cards.definition: the amount or quantity of something produced or traded
cards.example: Order volume typically increases during the holiday season.

cards.definition: done by choice without being forced or required
cards.example: Several employees voluntarily signed up for the weekend shift.

cards.definition: a ticket or document exchangeable for goods or services
cards.example: Conference attendees received a voucher for a complimentary lunch.

cards.definition: to choose not to enforce a right or requirement
cards.example: The bank agreed to waive the late payment fee.

cards.definition: a large building used for storing goods before distribution
cards.example: The new warehouse can hold twice as much inventory.

cards.definition: relating to selling goods in large quantities at lower prices
cards.example: We purchase office supplies at wholesale prices to save money.

cards.definition: the total number of workers in a company or region
cards.example: The company plans to expand its workforce by fifteen percent.

cards.definition: the amount of work assigned to a person or team
cards.example: Additional staff were hired to manage the increased workload.

cards.definition: worth the time, money, or effort spent
cards.example: The training program proved to be a worthwhile investment.

cards.definition: to finish or conclude something
cards.example: Let us wrap up the meeting so everyone can return to work.

cards.definition: happening once every year or per year
cards.example: All employees must complete a yearly performance evaluation.

cards.definition: up to this point in time; so far
cards.example: The revised budget has not yet been approved by management.

cards.definition: the total amount produced or the return on an investment
cards.example: The bond offers an annual yield of four percent.

cards.definition: a designated area for a specific purpose or activity
cards.example: Deliveries must be made through the designated loading zone.

cards.definition: regulations controlling how land or property may be used
cards.example: The zoning laws prevented commercial development in that area.
